claims.title: Many plan subcommand handlers call bare load_plan() ignoring the state-derived plan path
claims.detail: Confirmed across all inspected files. reorder_handlers.py:49, queue_render.py:179, commit_log_handlers.py:193, cmd.py:102 and :165, cluster_handlers.py (9 call sites: _cmd_cluster_create:94, _cmd_cluster_add:117, _cmd_cluster_remove:169, _cmd_cluster_delete:197, _cmd_cluster_reorder:363, _cmd_cluster_show:401, _cmd_cluster_list:494, _cmd_cluster_update:532, _cmd_cluster_merge:565), and override_handlers.py:125 (describe) and :150 (note) all call bare load_plan() without passing the state-derived path.

claims.title: Only skip, unskip, reopen in override_handlers.py correctly use _plan_file_for_state()
claims.detail: cmd_plan_skip (lines 241-243), cmd_plan_unskip (lines 310-312), and cmd_plan_reopen (lines 354-355) all correctly derive plan_file from runtime.state_path via _plan_file_for_state(). No other handlers do this consistently.

claims.title: Divergence is only observable with non-default --state paths
claims.detail: When --state is the default (.desloppify/state.json), plan_path_for_state() resolves to .desloppify/plan.json which equals PLAN_FILE. Divergence only occurs when a user passes a --state flag pointing to a different directory (e.g. --state /some/other/dir/state.json). In that case, skip/unskip/reopen read the co-located plan.json while all other handlers silently read/write the default plan.json, creating an inconsistency in plan state across a single command invocation.

notes: The inconsistency is real and systematic. While the practical blast radius is limited to non-default --state configurations, the pattern is wrong and creates a subtle footgun: users with custom state paths (e.g. per-language state files) will see skip/unskip correctly targeting the language-specific plan while all other commands target the default plan. Significance ~5/10: real bug, modest real-world impact. Fix complete across all plan subcommand handlers in PR #406 (describe/note/reorder/queue/commit-log/show/reset/9 cluster handlers) and PR #407 (move, resolve, focus, scan_gate, reflect, organize, record_triage_stage).
